title: Sheinbaum Named to TIME 100 Most Influential People of 2026
detail: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum was named to TIME magazine's annual list of the 100 Most Influential People in the World for 2026, published on April 19. The recognition comes as Sheinbaum navigates Mexico through one of its most complex multi-front diplomatic and security challenges: the CJNG post-Mencho succession crisis, the July 1 USMCA joint review deadline, escalating tensions with the Trump administration over ICE detention deaths and cartel FTO designations, and security preparations for the 2026 FIFA World Cup opening June 11. Sheinbaum is Mexico's first female president, inaugurated October 1, 2024. The TIME recognition highlights her profile internationally as a leader pushing back against Trump administration pressure while sustaining Mexico's economic relationship with the US.

media.caption: TIME 100 Most Influential People 2026 — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum named to the list

title: US-Mexico USMCA Bilateral Talks Continue — Economy Secretary Ebrard Leads Mexican Delegation
detail: The next round of US-Mexico USMCA pre-review bilateral talks proceeded on April 19 in Mexico City, following USTR Greer's April 16 visit. Economy Secretary Marcelo Ebrard leads the Mexican delegation in formal consultations with the US Trade Representative's office. The July 1, 2026 formal review deadline creates urgency — Mexico is seeking to maintain USMCA protections for its automobile exports, agricultural products, and manufactured goods. Under the current structure, USMCA-qualifying goods are exempt from the Trump administration's 25% tariff on Mexican imports, while non-compliant goods face the full tariff. The review's outcome will shape Mexico's economic trajectory for the next decade and represents one of the most consequential US-Mexico negotiations since the original 1994 NAFTA.

media.caption: USMCA bilateral review talks between US and Mexico continue as July 1 deadline approaches
